title: The History of Don Quixote, Volume 2, Part 23
summaries: "The History of Don Quixote, Volume 2, Part 23" by Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra is part of a novel originally published in two parts in 1605 and 1615. This section continues the adventures of Alonso Quijano, a hidalgo who loses his sanity from reading chivalric romances and becomes the knight-errant Don Quixote. Accompanied by his squire Sancho Panza, he journeys through Spain mistaking windmills for giants and inns for castles, unable to distinguish reality from his romantic delusions of knighthood. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
subjects: Spain -- Social life and customs -- 16th century -- Fiction
